# Setup and execution tracks {#sec-setup}

This chapter takes you from a clean machine to a working checkout, explains the
five reproduction tracks in detail, and describes the toolchain the heavy tracks
need. If you only want the paper's tables and figures, the `quick` and `verify`
tracks in the [quick start](index.qmd#quick-start) are all you need; nothing
here is required reading before running them.

## The self-locating path model

Every script derives its paths from the package root, which it finds by walking
up from its own location until it sees `pvstackr-replication.Rproj`. There is no
`setwd()` to edit and no absolute path baked into any file. In practice this
means:

- You may run a script from the package root, from a subdirectory, or through
  `run_all.R`, and it will resolve the same inputs and outputs.
- **Inputs are read-only.** Source code lives in `code/`, `stan/`, and
  `tests/`; shipped evidence lives in `data/precomputed/`; expected values live
  in `verification/expected/`. Nothing in a normal run writes to these.
- **Outputs are disposable.** Rebuilt tables and figures go to `output/`;
  verification reports go to `verification/reports/`. Both are safe to delete and
  regenerate.

The helper that implements this, `code/helpers/paths.R`, also refuses absolute
or parent-escaping configuration paths, so a stray `--config /etc/...` cannot
redirect a run outside the package.

## Restore the pinned environment

The package pins its dependencies with [renv](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/).
On R 4.3 or newer, restore them once from the package root:

```sh
Rscript -e 'if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) \
  install.packages("renv", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org"); \
  renv::restore(prompt = FALSE)'
```

`renv.lock` records the exact package versions the analysis was run under. The
`quick`, `intermediate`, and `verify` tracks need only CRAN packages; the
`simulation` and `pisa` full paths additionally need a working CmdStan
toolchain (below). A diagnostic preflight reports what is present without
installing anything:

```sh
Rscript code/00_setup.R --check      # R version, per-track package readiness, toolchain
Rscript run_all.R --help             # the full command-line interface
```

::: {.callout-note}
The frozen CI environment uses R 4.6.0, but the enforced minimum is R 4.3.0
(`code/00_setup.R`). A newer patch release changes nothing in the reported
numbers; it can change only PDF byte-metadata, which the verifier deliberately
does not treat as scientific content (see @sec-repro and @sec-faq).
:::

## The five tracks in detail {#sec-tracks-detail}

`run_all.R` dispatches to one of five track entrypoints declared in
`config/defaults.yml`. The mapping is read live below:

```{r}
#| label: tbl-track-map
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Track entrypoints and default modes, read live from `config/defaults.yml`."
root <- getwd()
while (!file.exists(file.path(root, "pvstackr-replication.Rproj")) && dirname(root) != root) root <- dirname(root)
cfg_path <- file.path(root, "config", "defaults.yml")
if (requireNamespace("yaml", quietly = TRUE) && file.exists(cfg_path)) {
  cfg <- yaml::read_yaml(cfg_path)
  tr  <- cfg$tracks
  tab <- data.frame(
    Track        = names(tr),
    Entrypoint   = vapply(tr, function(x) x$entrypoint,   character(1)),
    `Default mode` = vapply(tr, function(x) x$default_mode, character(1)),
    check.names  = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
  knitr::kable(tab, row.names = FALSE)
} else {
  cat("config/defaults.yml not found; run from the package root.\n")
}
```

### `quick` --- rebuild the exhibits (default)

Rebuilds every table and figure in the paper and supplement from the committed
`data/precomputed/` layer. No Stan, no PISA microdata, minutes on a laptop.

```sh
Rscript run_all.R --track quick
```

### `intermediate` --- recompute the transformations

Recomputes the Rubin pooling, the CCC calibration targets, the aggregation, and
the reporting gates from the shipped draw and target objects, so you can inspect
the statistical machinery of @sec-methods without refitting anything.

```sh
Rscript run_all.R --track intermediate
```

### `simulation` --- the synthetic parity study

`--mode smoke` validates the archive, the seed contract, and the adversarial
controls without starting Stan; adding `--execute` runs one live reduced
replication. `--mode full` writes the persistent 4,000-task manifest and, with
`--execute`, runs (or resumes) the complete study --- parallel hardware only.

```sh
Rscript run_all.R --track simulation --dry-run -- --mode smoke   # print the resolved command
Rscript run_all.R --track simulation -- --mode smoke             # validate the archive path
Rscript run_all.R --track simulation -- --mode smoke --execute   # one live reduced replay
Rscript run_all.R --track simulation -- --mode full              # 4,000-task preflight
```

### `pisa` --- the empirical analysis

`--mode cached` rebuilds the empirical evidence from sanitized draw and target
objects (no microdata). `--mode full` refits the 22 MCMC models and requires
*authorized local OECD data* (see @sec-pisa-data).

```sh
Rscript run_all.R --track pisa -- --mode cached
Rscript run_all.R --track pisa -- --mode full            # 22-task preflight
Rscript run_all.R --track pisa -- --mode full --execute  # refit A and C; re-audit B
```

### `verify` --- audit the package

Checks the manifest, schemas, numeric anchors, reporting gates, and publication
hygiene. This is the track a reviewer runs; @sec-repro describes each component.

```sh
Rscript run_all.R --track verify
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Always dry-run a heavy path first (`--dry-run`), and remember that a heavy task
is *complete only when its expected-key manifest is complete*. Partial or
interrupted output is never promoted into a manuscript aggregate --- the
aggregator checks the full task grid before it accepts anything (@sec-simulation).
:::

## The heavy-track toolchain

`simulation --mode full` and `pisa --mode full` fit models with
[CmdStan](https://mc-stan.org/) through `cmdstanr` and `brms`. Before an
`--execute` run the setup preflight checks for a resolvable CmdStan
installation, a C++ compiler (`c++`), `make`, and --- importantly --- a
**space-free temporary directory**. CmdStan's build step fails on paths that
contain spaces, so if your `TMPDIR` contains a space, set a clean one first:

```sh
export TMPDIR="$HOME/.pvstackr_tmp"   # any space-free path
mkdir -p "$TMPDIR"
```

The other tracks never touch this toolchain.

## The software boundary {#sec-software-boundary}

This repository ships a **frozen, portable copy** of the exact analysis engine
that produced the paper's random-intercept results, under `code/01_core/` and
`stan/`. The separately maintained
[**pvstackr**](https://github.com/joonho112/pvstackr) package (version 0.1.0) is
conceptually related software and documents the general workflow, but its
current public API does *not* implement this exact weighted random-intercept
execution path, and it is **not a dependency** of this package. Keeping the two
apart prevents two misleading claims: that pvstackr 0.1.0 generated the archived
results, or that they can be reproduced by substituting its current API. The
mapping between the two is recorded in `docs/software-lineage.md` and
`verification/pvstackr-compatibility.csv`.

For installation troubleshooting and failure recovery, see
`docs/getting-started.md` and `docs/troubleshooting.md`.
